title: Fermented Gooey Mediterranean Bacon Tagine
description: A rich and indulgent Mediterranean dish featuring crispy bacon lardon, fermented flavors, and gooey textures, all kosher-friendly. This recipe combines the warmth of bhuna spices with the comfort of a slow-cooked tagine.

ingredients.name: fermented Mediterranean vegetable mix
ingredients.notes: such as pickled turnips and cauliflower

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, cook the bacon lardon over medium heat until crispy, then set aside.
In the same pot, add the olive oil, chopped onion, and minced garlic. Cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the bhuna spice mix and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the fermented Mediterranean vegetable mix, chicken broth, dried apricots, and honey. Stir to combine.
Return the cooked bacon lardon to the pot and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise for 2 hours, or until the flavors have melded together and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly. Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
